tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Aericura is a Gaulish goddess associated with the underworld and fertility.
  • She is often linked with prosperity and life after death.
  • She represents both death and rebirth.
  • She is sometimes paired with the god Dis Pater.
  • She symbolizes the cycle of life and the afterlife.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Afterlife Control – Governs the realm of the dead.
  • Fertility – Represents growth and renewal.
  • Wealth – Associated with prosperity.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Worshipped in Gaul and parts of Europe.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Aengus is the Irish god of love, youth, and poetic inspiration.
  • He is known for his charm and beauty.
  • He is associated with romance and dreams.
  • He lives in the magical palace of Brú na Bóinne.
  • He symbolizes eternal youth and desire.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Love Manipulation – Inspires romance.
  • Youth – Represents eternal beauty.
  • Dream Influence – Controls dreams.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Airmed is the goddess of healing and herbal medicine.
  • She is known for her deep knowledge of plants.
  • She restores life and health.
  • She represents natural healing.
  • She symbolizes medical wisdom.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Healing – Cures wounds.
  • Herbal Knowledge – Uses plants for medicine.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Amaethon is a Welsh god of agriculture and farming.
  • He represents growth and cultivation.
  • He is associated with fertility of the land.
  • He plays a role in mythological battles.
  • He symbolizes sustenance and food production.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Crop Growth – Enhances agriculture.
  • Fertility – Supports land productivity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Andraste is a Celtic goddess of war and victory.
  • She was worshipped by warriors.
  • She represents triumph in battle.
  • She symbolizes strength and courage.
  • She is associated with leadership in war.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• War Power – Strength in battle.
  • Victory – Ensures success.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Arawn is the ruler of the Welsh underworld, Annwn.
  • He governs souls and the afterlife.
  • He represents mystery and death.
  • He is depicted as fair and just.
  • He symbolizes the unseen world.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Arduinna is a goddess of forests and wild animals.
  • She represents wilderness and nature.
  • She is often depicted with a boar.
  • She protects forests.
  • She symbolizes untamed nature.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Badb is a war goddess associated with fate and battle.
  • She appears as a crow on battlefields.
  • She influences victory and death.
  • She is part of the Morrigan.
  • She symbolizes chaos in war.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Banba is a goddess representing Ireland.
  • She protects the land.
  • She symbolizes sovereignty.
  • She is one of three land goddesses.
  • She represents national identity.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • One of Ireland’s ancient names comes from her.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Belenus is a sun god associated with light and healing.
  • He brings warmth and health.
  • He is linked to fire festivals.
  • He represents life energy.
  • He symbolizes renewal.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Belisama is a goddess of fire and light.
  • She represents brightness and brilliance.
  • She is associated with crafts.
  • She symbolizes inspiration.
  • She is linked with creativity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Blodeuwedd is a woman created from flowers.
  • She represents beauty and nature.
  • She later transforms into an owl.
  • She symbolizes transformation.
  • She is part of Welsh myths.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Bodb Derg is a king of the Tuatha Dé Danann.
  • He rules over fairy beings.
  • He represents leadership.
  • He is associated with magic.
  • He symbolizes authority.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Bran the Blessed is a giant king and hero in Welsh mythology.
  • He is known for his immense size and strength.
  • He protects Britain and its people.
  • Even after death, his head continues to guard the land.
  • He symbolizes sacrifice and protection.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Super Strength – Immense physical power.
  • Protection – Guards the land.
  • Endurance – Survives beyond death.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • His head was buried to protect Britain.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Brigid is one of the most important Irish goddesses.
  • She is associated with fire, healing, and poetry.
  • She represents creativity and wisdom.
  • She is connected to the festival of Imbolc.
  • She symbolizes inspiration and life.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Fire – Represents energy.
  • Healing – Restores health.
  • Inspiration – Enhances creativity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Camulus is a Celtic war god worshipped in Gaul.
  • He represents battle and strength.
  • He is associated with warriors.
  • He symbolizes courage and power.
  • He is sometimes linked with Mars.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Cernunnos is the horned god of nature and animals.
  • He is associated with forests and wildlife.
  • He represents fertility and abundance.
  • He is depicted with antlers.
  • He symbolizes the wild world.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • One of the most iconic Celtic gods.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Cerridwen is a goddess of magic, transformation, and knowledge.
  • She owns a cauldron of wisdom.
  • She represents change and rebirth.
  • She is a powerful sorceress.
  • She symbolizes knowledge and power.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Clíodhna is a goddess of the sea and beauty.
  • She is associated with waves and the coastline.
  • She represents charm and attraction.
  • She is connected with fairy realms.
  • She symbolizes mystery and allure.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Coventina is a goddess of springs and wells.
  • She is associated with healing waters.
  • She represents purity.
  • She protects water sources.
  • She symbolizes renewal.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Danu is the mother goddess of the Tuatha Dé Danann.
  • She represents creation and fertility.
  • She is a source of life.
  • She symbolizes abundance.
  • She is one of the most important deities.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Dian Cecht is the god of healing and medicine.
  • He restores warriors in battle.
  • He is associated with magical healing wells.
  • He represents knowledge of medicine.
  • He symbolizes restoration.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Dylan ail Don is a sea god associated with the ocean.
  • He is connected to waves and tides.
  • He represents fluidity and motion.
  • He is part of Welsh mythology.
  • He symbolizes natural forces.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Epona is a goddess associated with horses.
  • She protects travelers.
  • She represents movement and strength.
  • She is widely worshipped.
  • She symbolizes freedom.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Eriu is one of the goddesses representing Ireland.
  • She gives the land its name.
  • She symbolizes sovereignty.
  • She protects the land.
  • She represents identity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Esus is a mysterious Gaulish god associated with strength and possibly war.
  • He is depicted cutting trees.
  • He represents power and force.
  • He is linked to sacrifice rituals.
  • He symbolizes strength and endurance.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • One of the triad of Gaulish gods.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Flidais is a goddess of forests, animals, and fertility.
  • She is associated with deer and wild creatures.
  • She represents abundance in nature.
  • She provides nourishment and sustenance.
  • She symbolizes harmony with wildlife.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Known as a provider of milk from magical cattle.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Fodla is one of the goddesses representing Ireland.
  • She symbolizes the land and its identity.
  • She is connected with sovereignty.
  • She protects the territory.
  • She represents ancient heritage.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Her name is an old poetic name for Ireland.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Goibniu is the divine smith of the Tuatha Dé Danann.
  • He creates powerful weapons.
  • His weapons never miss their target.
  • He represents craftsmanship and skill.
  • He also provides a magical feast of immortality.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Part of a trio of craftsmen gods.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Grannus is a god associated with healing and the sun.
  • He is linked to thermal springs.
  • He represents health and vitality.
  • He is worshipped for cures.
  • He symbolizes life energy.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Gwydion is a powerful magician and trickster figure.
  • He uses magic and cunning.
  • He creates beings through enchantment.
  • He represents intelligence.
  • He symbolizes magical knowledge.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Lenus is a god associated with both healing and war.
  • He protects warriors.
  • He restores health.
  • He represents strength and recovery.
  • He symbolizes dual nature.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Llŷr is a sea god and father of important figures like Bran.
  • He represents the ocean’s power.
  • He symbolizes authority and lineage.
  • He is connected with maritime forces.
  • He embodies deep waters.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Lleu is a hero god associated with skill and light.
  • He is known for his unusual death conditions.
  • He represents precision and intelligence.
  • He is a warrior and leader.
  • He symbolizes resilience.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Lugh is one of the most powerful Celtic gods.
  • He is a master of all skills.
  • He is associated with the sun.
  • He is a warrior and leader.
  • He symbolizes excellence.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Macha is a goddess linked to war and sovereignty.
  • She is associated with horses.
  • She represents strength and power.
  • She plays roles in multiple myths.
  • She symbolizes land and battle.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Manannan mac Lir is a powerful sea god and magician.
  • He controls the ocean and weather.
  • He guides souls to the afterlife.
  • He represents mystery and travel.
  • He symbolizes the sea’s magic.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Maponos is a god of youth and vitality.
  • He is associated with music and joy.
  • He represents eternal youth.
  • He symbolizes happiness.
  • He is linked to Apollo.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Math ap Mathonwy is a powerful king and magician.
  • He rules Gwynedd.
  • He possesses strong magical abilities.
  • He represents authority and wisdom.
  • He symbolizes leadership.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Must keep his feet in a maiden’s lap.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Midir is a god of the Otherworld, associated with magic and immortality.
  • He resides in a supernatural realm.
  • He is connected with love and transformation.
  • He represents mystery and eternal life.
  • He symbolizes the connection between worlds.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• The Morrigan is a powerful goddess of war and fate.
  • She appears as a crow on battlefields.
  • She influences victory and destiny.
  • She can shapeshift.
  • She represents chaos and prophecy.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Often seen as a trio of goddesses.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nemain is a war goddess associated with frenzy and panic.
  • She causes confusion among warriors.
  • She represents chaos in battle.
  • She is linked with the Morrigan.
  • She symbolizes fear and disorder.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nodens is a god associated with healing and hunting.
  • He is linked to the sea and dogs.
  • He protects and restores health.
  • He represents recovery.
  • He symbolizes strength.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nuada is the king of the Tuatha Dé Danann.
  • He is known for his silver arm.
  • He represents leadership and justice.
  • He is a warrior king.
  • He symbolizes authority.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Ogma is the god of language, writing, and strength.
  • He created the Ogham script.
  • He is both a warrior and a scholar.
  • He represents intelligence.
  • He symbolizes communication.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Rhiannon is a goddess associated with horses and magic.
  • She represents sovereignty and power.
  • She is linked to the Otherworld.
  • She symbolizes grace.
  • She plays a key role in Welsh myths.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Rosmerta is a goddess of wealth and abundance.
  • She is associated with prosperity.
  • She provides nourishment.
  • She represents growth.
  • She symbolizes fortune.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Sequana is the goddess of the River Seine.
  • She is associated with healing waters.
  • She represents purification.
  • She protects rivers.
  • She symbolizes life.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Sirona is a goddess of healing and fertility.
  • She is associated with springs.
  • She represents health and renewal.
  • She symbolizes life.
  • She is worshipped for cures.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Sucellus is a god of agriculture and forests.
  • He carries a hammer symbolizing power.
  • He represents growth and nourishment.
  • He is associated with prosperity.
  • He symbolizes strength.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Sulis is a goddess of healing waters.
  • She is associated with hot springs.
  • She represents purification.
  • She protects sacred waters.
  • She symbolizes renewal.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Taranis is the god of thunder and the sky.
  • He controls storms and lightning.
  • He represents power and force.
  • He is associated with the wheel symbol.
  • He symbolizes cosmic energy.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Teutates is a protector god of tribes and people.
  • He is associated with war and defense.
  • He safeguards communities.
  • He represents unity.
  • He symbolizes strength in numbers.
